text.title: Backlog Grooming and Roadmapping Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Backlog Grooming and Roadmapping application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Grooming MUST run at a fixed weekly cadence; ad-hoc grooming rejected.
P0 backlog MUST contain ≤3 items at any time.
rule.rationale: More than 3 P0s means nothing is P0.

rule.statement: AI-generated RICE/MoSCoW scores MUST be marked [estimate]; un-flagged AI scores rejected.
rule.rationale: Un-flagged AI scores get treated as data instead of hypothesis.

rule.statement: Roadmap MUST use Now/Next/Later horizons; date-based roadmaps rejected for solo/small-team.
rule.rationale: Date-based roadmaps lie within one cycle.

rule.statement: Every score MUST carry a one-line rationale; un-rationalised scores rejected.
rule.rationale: Un-rationalised scores cannot be defended or re-evaluated.
